feat: Multi-user YouTube auth with auto-refresh

Global default account (family Premium) + per-user overrides.
Resolution: get_auth('youtube', user_id='jake') → jake's → global.

Google refresh tokens last indefinitely — one-time login only.
Admin: set-global endpoint, per-user start/complete, user_id param.
MediaView: global account card, per-user list, Set as Default button.
91 tests passing (25 new: multi-user, fallback, auto-refresh, endpoints).

commit.message:
Fix the accuracy issue by update celu formula based on spec (#33500)

### Details:
- The implementation didn't align with [ONNX
spec](https://onnx.ai/onnx/operators/onnx__Celu.html).
```
max(0,x) + min(0,alpha*(exp(x/alpha)-1))
```
 

### Tickets:
 - [CVS-178958](https://jira.devtools.intel.com/browse/CVS-178958)

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
TECHDEBT-35: Fix VultronEvent to carry rich domain objects

Replace the flat ID/type string fields on VultronEvent with 7 rich
VultronObject | None fields (object_, target, context, origin,
inner_object, inner_target, inner_context) that mirror the AS2 Activity
structure at the core layer.

Changes:
- events/base.py: Replace 14 flat fields with 7 VultronObject fields;
  add derived @property for all old names (backward compat); object_
  uses trailing underscore because 'object' is a Python built-in
- events/_mixins.py: Add rich 'foo' property alongside each 'foo_id'
  property; properties return self.object_, self.target, etc. with
  domain-typed hints under TYPE_CHECKING
- event subclasses (report, case, embargo, note, status, case_participant,
  actor): Remove explicit typed domain fields (report: VultronReport,
  case: VultronCase, etc.) — now provided by mixin properties
- extractor.py: Update _build_domain_kwargs() to use object_= instead of
  per-semantic keys; add _to_domain_obj() helper; remove all flat _id/_type
  kwargs from constructor call; import VultronObject
- test_report_use_cases.py: Update 3 event constructors to use object_=

All 988 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(release): harden media runtime and publish packaging

Why: ingest outputs could overwrite each other, audio/video capability checks drifted between runtime and providers, and the publish pipeline could fail lint or ship stale dist artifacts.

Why: this keeps the validated 0.2.1 release changes together and makes the npm publish path reproducible.

commit.message:
feat: Complete OS image builder for tablet satellite

Rewrites build-image.sh from ISO-remaster to full debootstrap+chroot
OS builder. The output is a ready-to-flash image — no installer runs
on the tablet. Flash, boot, connect WiFi, avatar appears.

build-image.sh (12-phase OS builder):
  - debootstrap Ubuntu Noble (24.04) minimal rootfs
  - Installs Openbox + Chromium kiosk (no full DE)
  - Installs linux-surface kernel for Surface Go support
  - Pre-installs Atlas satellite agent + Python venv
  - Captive portal for touchscreen WiFi setup on first boot
  - mDNS announce service (avahi-publish)
  - First-boot service for unique hostname/satellite ID
  - Squashfs + GRUB bootable ISO output
  - Optional --raw disk image format
  - Cleanup trap, SHA256 checksum generation

.github/workflows/build-tablet-image.yml:
  - Mirrors Pi image builder pattern (build-satellite-image.yml)
  - Triggers: release, monthly schedule, manual dispatch
  - Option to include/exclude linux-surface kernel
  - Uploads artifact + GitHub Release with checksums
  - 60-minute timeout

install-tablet.sh retained as alternative for existing Ubuntu.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(fitness-functions): avoid ENOBUFS on large git diffs (#40648)

## Description
Fix the `ENOBUFS` caused by our fitness functions failing on large
commits (technically just caused by git commands with large amounts of
output) by routing fitness-function git commands through a shared helper
with a larger maxBuffer, so large diffs do not fail (as often?) while
being collected.

## Changelog
CHANGELOG entry: null
## Manual testing steps
1. Create or select a branch with a large staged diff or a large branch
diff.
2. Run the fitness functions through the normal pre-commit or pre-push
workflow.
3. Confirm diff collection succeeds without ENOBUFS failures.
## Pre-merge author checklist
- [x] I've followed [MetaMask Contributor
Docs](https://github.com/MetaMask/contributor-docs) and [MetaMask
Extension Coding
Standards](https://github.com/MetaMask/metamask-extension/blob/main/.github/guidelines/CODING_GUIDELINES.md).
- [x] I've completed the PR template to the best of my ability
- [x] I've included tests if applicable
- [ ] I've documented my code using [JSDoc](https://jsdoc.app/) format
if applicable
- [x] I've applied the right labels on the PR (see [labeling
guidelines](https://github.com/MetaMask/metamask-extension/blob/main/docs/labeling-guidelines.md)).
Not required for external contributors.
***
Note
Low Risk
Overview
Updates pre-commit and pre-push fitness-function diff generation to use
execFileSync with a shared 50 MB maxBuffer, reducing failures on large
diffs and avoiding ENOBUFS errors during hook execution.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Improve controls: number clears after placement, cell stays selected

New interaction flow:
- Tap cell (no number armed) → selects cell
- Tap number (cell selected) → fills cell, number clears, cell stays selected
- Tap number (no cell) → arms number
- Tap cell (number armed) → fills cell, number clears, cell stays selected
- Notes mode: number stays selected after use for rapid multi-candidate entry
- Tap same cell again (no number) → deselects

Same flow applied to creator mode.

commit.message:
fix: prevent remove() crash on stale undefined/zero slots in all PQ types

- PriorityQueue.remove(): add node && null-guard in findIndex (fixes
  TypeError crash when _elements contains undefined slots from prior
  dequeue/pop or _grow calls)
- TypedPriorityQueue.remove(): replace findIndex with bounded loop
  limited to _size to avoid matching stale zero-filled slots
- StableTypedPriorityQueue.remove(): same bounded-loop fix
- TypedPriorityQueue.indexOf() and StableTypedPriorityQueue.indexOf():
  same bounded-loop fix for consistency
- Add regression tests for dequeue-then-remove across all 4 PQ types
- Regenerate docs

Three unguarded direct em_state = EM.ACTIVE assignments in vultron/core/
now have explicit machine guards:

  raises VultronConflictError. Consistent with SvcProposeEmbargoUseCase
  and SvcTerminateEmbargoUseCase.

  transition; proceeds regardless (documented state-sync override).

All rm_state=RM.XXX in core are constructor args for new status objects
(initial-state constructions, not transitions). The append_rm_state()
guard already enforces is_valid_rm_transition() for all RM mutation paths.

commit.message:
fix: remove traceparent from metadata to enable HTTP span nesting

When traceparent/tracestate were in metadata, the AzureAIOpenTelemetryTracer
extracted them via _extract_trace_headers() and called use_propagated_context(),
creating a NEW root context that bypassed the FastAPI HTTP span. Now the
callback inherits the OTel context from the FastAPI ASGI instrumentor,
so the HTTP request span becomes the tree root.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: EVE wormhole type lookup from SDE + ESI prefill (#8)

* fix: far-side scout return mass is always cold

Scouts return through the wormhole without activating MWD, so their
return mass should always be coldMass regardless of how they entered.
Previously selecting 'Hot' used hotMass for the return budget, causing
the rolling plan to over-reserve mass for the scout's exit pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: EVE SDE wormhole type lookup and ESI prefill

- Add scripts/update-sde.py: downloads EVE SDE ZIP (ETag-gated),
  extracts group-988 wormhole types from fsd/typeIDs.yaml, generates
  data/wormhole-types.json and data/sde-metadata.json
- Add data/wormhole-types.json: 130 wormhole type designations→typeIDs
  (initial seed from ESI group 988; re-generate with update-sde.py)
- Add data/sde-metadata.json: tracks SDE ETag, SHA256, and count
- Update build-offline.py: embeds wormhole-types.json as
  window.WH_TYPE_DATA for offline/desktop HTML builds
- Add typeahead Wormhole Type dropdown to Setup config:
  - datalist input filtered by typing; lists all known WH designations
  - On selection, fetches ESI /universe/types/{id} and prefills
    Total Mass (attr 1383) and Wormhole Size (attr 1385 mapped to
    small/medium/large/xl/xxl) — fields remain editable
  - ⓘ badge appears if typed value doesn't match any known type
  - Animated ⋯ spinner while ESI fetch is in progress
- Combined header display: "{TypeName} — {Name}" or just one if only
  one is set
- wormhole.typeName persisted to localStorage with other WH config
- .gitignore: add *.zip (SDE download) and __pycache__/

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: replace SDE download with ESI-based wormhole type list

Remove the SDE download script and static JSON files entirely.
On page load, fetch the wormhole type list directly from ESI:
  1. GET /universe/groups/988/ → type IDs
  2. POST /universe/names/ → names for all 130 type IDs at once
Strip 'Wormhole ' prefix, sort alphabetically, cache in localStorage
(key: eve-whr-wh-types-v1) with a 7-day TTL before re-fetching.

When a type is selected, /universe/types/{id}/ is still used to
prefill Total Mass and Wormhole Size using named constants:
  ESI_ATTR_WH_MAX_STABLE_MASS (1383) → Total Mass
  ESI_ATTR_WH_MAX_JUMP_MASS   (1385) → Wormhole Size (mapped to app sizes)

All ESI attribute IDs are now module-level constants with comments
so the mapping is clear to future developers.

Removed:
  scripts/update-sde.py
  data/wormhole-types.json
  data/sde-metadata.json

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: daily cache TTL for wormhole types + manual refresh button

- Change WH_TYPES_CACHE_TTL_MS from 7 days to 1 day so the type list
  stays current with EVE Online's daily downtime update cadence
- Add ↺ refresh button next to the Wormhole Type input to force a
  re-fetch from ESI (useful on patch days without waiting for cache expiry)
- Fix duplicate placeholder attribute on the type input
- Add .wh-type-refresh-btn CSS to keep the button compact

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: replace native datalist with custom combobox; add grouped wormhole types

The native <datalist> caused a Chrome bug where all option values displayed
as the currently-selected value after a selection was made.

Replace with a fully custom combobox:
- Dropdown opens on focus/type, closes on blur/escape/select
- Arrow key navigation through grouped results (Up/Down/Enter/Escape)
- @mousedown.prevent on items ensures blur fires AFTER click
- Groups wormhole types by destination class using ESI dogma attr 1457
  (296=C1, 297=C2, …, 301=C6, 302=HS, 303=LS, 304=NS, 305=Thera, etc.)
- Each item shows the type code in monospace + size label (Frigate / Medium
  / Large / XL / Carrier+) aligned right
- Group headers are sticky within the scrollable dropdown

Data fetch updated to batch-fetch all 130 type details in groups of 30
parallel requests to retrieve destClass and maxJumpMass for grouping.
Cache format bumped to include destClass — old cache auto-invalidates.

Add ESI_ATTR_WH_DEST_CLASS = 1457 constant with explanation.
whTypeSearch ref decoupled from wormhole.typeName so Vue reactivity
does not interfere with the input value during dropdown rendering.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: remove 'Other' category, map all known wormhole destination classes

- Replace WH_DEST_CLASSES object with _whDestGroup() function that handles
  both simple 1:1 mappings and range-based lookups (Pochven uses a range
  since EVE assigns unique typeIds per Pochven system, all named C729)
- Filter out K162 (cls=0, no mass/size data), QA test holes (cls 628/629)
- Deduplicate by name: the 22+ C729 variants collapse to one entry
- Map cls 709 → Thera (E587, F135, Q063, V898)
- Map cls 731 → Drifter Hive (B735, C414, R259, S877, V928)
- Map cls 2925-3000 → Pochven (all C729 variants + X450, R081, F216, U372)
- Remaining exotic types (J377, J492, I078, L687, O546, cls>=3000) still
  appear as 'Other' since their destination classes are unconfirmed newer
  content — at least they're clearly labeled rather than being a mystery

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: correct wormhole type categorization and bust stale cache

- Add explicit mappings for J377 (cls 3128) and J492 (cls 3671) to LS — Low Security
- Add explicit mappings for I078 (4134), L687 (4136), O546 (4138) to Pochven
- Remove 'Other' catch-all fallback; unknown types are now filtered out entirely
- Bump STORAGE_WH_TYPES cache key to v2 to clear stale deduplication-era cache
  (fixes C729 and others appearing multiple times)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix One-Check Rule violation, Starmus microphone policy, upstream docs, and add threat signal propagation headers (#11)

* Initial plan

* Fix One-Check Rule violation, Starmus microphone policy, and upstream docs

Co-authored-by: MaximillianGroup <34328348+MaximillianGroup@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/Starisian-Technologies/sparxstar-event-horizon/sessions/ee566d50-2d5a-4a73-b30a-9a7220c306dd

* feat: add X-SPX threat signal propagation headers for Helios/Sirus

Co-authored-by: MaximillianGroup <34328348+MaximillianGroup@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/Starisian-Technologies/sparxstar-event-horizon/sessions/0f493b5e-abcf-4289-83c9-b4fad1d6ad83

* Update snippets/spx-horizon-rules.conf

@copilot - README  must say — FastCGI vs proxy_pass (THIS ONE IS IMPORTANT)

This is not a nitpick. This is a real architectural inconsistency.

Current problem

You wrote:

varnish_backend → can point to PHP-FPM socket

But your Nginx:

proxy_pass http://varnish_backend;

That means:

❌ You are speaking HTTP
❌ PHP-FPM speaks FastCGI

They are not compatible.

Copilot suggestion is correct

You must NOT imply PHP-FPM can be used behind proxy_pass.

Correct model (your stack)

You already use:

Cloudflare → Nginx → Varnish → Apache → PHP-FPM

So:

varnish_backend = HTTP ONLY
PHP-FPM is NEVER directly exposed to Event Horizon
Decision

✔ Accept the rewrite

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Max Barrett <34328348+MaximillianGroup@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix README FastCGI/proxy_pass inconsistency and risk-score key length comment

Co-authored-by: MaximillianGroup <34328348+MaximillianGroup@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/Starisian-Technologies/sparxstar-event-horizon/sessions/13bf49ce-87c1-443b-9193-1c534b95d917

* Scope X-SPX-* threat headers to dynamic locations only; avoid map eval on static bypass

refactor(TECHDEBT-32/32b): standardise on dl.save() in core; remove adapter imports

TECHDEBT-32 (research): audit of core/DataLayer boundary found two CS-05-001
violations — core trigger modules importing object_to_record from the adapter
layer — plus a redundant save_to_datalayer() helper in core/behaviors.
Analysis written to notes/datalayer-refactor.md.

TECHDEBT-32b (code fix): standardise all core persistence on dl.save(obj).
- Remove 'from vultron.adapters.driven.db_record import object_to_record'
  from triggers/embargo.py and triggers/_helpers.py.
- Replace 5 dl.update(..., object_to_record(...)) calls with dl.save(obj).
- Replace 4 save_to_datalayer(self.datalayer, obj) calls in BT nodes
  (case/nodes.py, report/nodes.py) with self.datalayer.save(obj).
- Delete save_to_datalayer() function from core/behaviors/helpers.py.
- Remove now-unused BaseModel import from helpers.py.
- Add TECHDEBT-32c to plan for remaining wire-imports-adapter violation
  in wire/as2/rehydration.py.

985 tests pass. Prerequisite for ACT-2 (PRIORITY-100 per-actor isolation).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add FastAPI HTTP instrumentation for e2e trace visibility

Adds opentelemetry-instrumentation-fastapi so inbound HTTP requests
produce server spans in App Insights. The HTTP span becomes the root
of the trace tree, with all GenAI agent/tool spans nested under it.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Update model tiers to Qwen3.5 — 9B and 27B as primary models

VRAM tiers now recommend:
- 48GB+: qwen3.5:27b (fast + thinking)
- 16-24GB: qwen3.5:9b fast + qwen3.5:27b thinking (27B fits Q4 at 16.5GB)
- 8GB: qwen3.5:9b
- 4GB: qwen3.5:9b (tight) or qwen2.5:3b fallback
- CPU: qwen2.5:1.5b

Installer offers: qwen3.5:27b, qwen3.5:9b, qwen2.5:7b, qwen2.5:1.5b
Plus community Claude-Opus-distilled variants (HuggingFace GGUF import)

262K native context on Qwen3.5 (up to 1M extended)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: e2e trace demo with traceparent propagation and custom metadata headers

- Install langchain-azure-ai from upstream main
- Extract metadata-* HTTP headers and inject as gen_ai.custom.* metadata
  so the AzureAIOpenTelemetryTracer sets them as span attributes
  (visible as customDimensions in Azure Monitor)
- Remove connection_string gate from create_langchain_callbacks so the
  tracer works with any pre-configured TracerProvider
- Validated: 21 spans, 16/21 carry incoming traceparent, custom headers
  appear on 12 agent spans

commit.message:
chore(vscode): replace deprecated workspace settings keys (#40823)

## **Description**

This PR updates the repository workspace settings to use the current VS
Code JavaScript and TypeScript setting keys.

The existing `.vscode/settings.json` file still referenced deprecated
language-specific keys. That creates avoidable warnings in VS Code and
makes the workspace configuration look stale even though the underlying
behavior is still valid.

This change replaces the deprecated keys with their modern `js/ts.*`
equivalents while preserving the existing workspace behavior:
- keep import module specifiers set to `relative`
- keep the workspace TypeScript SDK pointed at
`node_modules/typescript/lib`

[![Open in GitHub
Codespaces](https://github.com/codespaces/badge.svg)](https://codespaces.new/MetaMask/metamask-extension/pull/40823?quickstart=1)

## **Changelog**

CHANGELOG entry: null

<!-- ## **Related issues** -->

<!-- Fixes: -->

## **Manual testing steps**

1. Open the repository in VS Code.
2. Open `.vscode/settings.json` and confirm the deprecated
`javascript.preferences.importModuleSpecifier`,
`typescript.preferences.importModuleSpecifier`, and `typescript.tsdk`
keys are no longer present.
3. Confirm the workspace now uses
`js/ts.preferences.importModuleSpecifier` and `js/ts.tsdk.path` without
showing deprecation warnings.

<!-- Not applicable. -->

if applicable

guidelines](https://github.com/MetaMask/metamask-extension/blob/main/.github/guidelines/LABELING_GUIDELINES.md)).
Not required for external contributors.

## **Pre-merge reviewer checklist**

- [ ] I've manually tested the PR (e.g. pull and build branch, run the
app, test code being changed).
- [ ] I confirm that this PR addresses all acceptance criteria described
in the ticket it closes and includes the necessary testing evidence such
as recordings and or screenshots.
